title: 'Good Trouble' rallies return to protest Trump policies
shortDescription: Protesters are gathering nationwide for 'Good Trouble Lives On' rallies to advocate for civil and voting rights.

title: As Trump warns of election threats, independent agencies have been cut
shortDescription: Trump has reduced federal agencies that support election security, leaving states to defend their own systems.

title: Trump claims US election vulnerabilities in primetime speech: 5 takeaways
shortDescription: The president said America's elections are 'worse than a third-world country' after making broad claims about China meddling in the 2020 contest

title: On the cusp of midterms, Trump's rambling election speech looks backward
shortDescription: In President Trump's primetime address, the main topic wasn't the economy or war in Iran. It was his grievances about the only election he has lost.

title: Newsom calls Trump's address "the ramblings of a mad king"
shortDescription: California Gov. Gavin Newsom responded to President Donald Trump's address, calling it "the ramblings of a mad king" and urging voters to turn out in the 2026 election.

title: Trump questions California elections in integrity speech
shortDescription: Gov. Gavin Newsom, California governor hopeful Xavier Becerra and more responded to the president's remarks about elections and voting in the U.S.

title: 8 key events in Trump's 2020 election challenge
shortDescription: From election fraud claims to Jan. 6, here's a look at eight key efforts Trump and his allies made to challenge the 2020 election.

title: Trump claims cover-up of China election meddling
shortDescription: In a primetime address to the nation, President Trump claimed the so-called "deep state" hid evidence of China election meddling.

title: Trump slams ABC, NBC for not carrying his speech
shortDescription: President Trump called for the FCC to revoke ABC's and NBC's television licenses for choosing not to broadcast his primetime address.

title: Trump claims elections vulnerable to interference
shortDescription: President Trump said Americans have for years been "blatantly lied to" about the security of election infrastructure, including voting machines.

title: President Trump addresses nation in primetime speech: See the photos
shortDescription: President Trump addressed the nation on election security and voting issues ahead of November’s midterm elections.

title: US attorney fired the same day as appointment. Trump news today
shortDescription: Karoline Leavitt is back from maternity leave and a teleprompter operator was fired. What's happening in President Donald Trump's world today.

title: The House GOP is forging ahead with a SAVE Act plan. It may be doomed.
shortDescription: Vice President JD Vance has embraced the House GOP's plan to pass SAVE America. One Republican, however, is calling the effort a "waste of time."

title: Trump approval stuck at 37% in new poll
shortDescription: Trump's approval remains stuck in the 30s as a new poll shows growing voter frustration with the economy and the Iran conflict.

title: Raising old grievances, Trump says US elections like a 'third-world country'
shortDescription: President Donald Trump, who has long baselessly claimed that he won the 2020 election, cast new doubt on election security months before the midterms.

title: 'Abrasive,' Epstein accuser says of meeting with Trump AG pick Blanche
shortDescription: Acting Attorney General Todd Blanche met with Jeffrey Epstein accusers, hours after his permanent AG nomination was threatened over the issue.

title: Ossoff slams Trump over 'sore loser' speech
shortDescription: Sen. Jon Ossoff criticized President Trump's upcoming speech on the 2020 election, calling him "the world's most famous sore loser."

title: WH employee accused of betting on Trump speeches
shortDescription: A White House teleprompter operator was accused of making money betting on President Trump's speeches was placed on unpaid administrative leave.

title: White House employee on leave for betting on Trump speeches
shortDescription: Press Secretary Karoline Leavitt confirmed that federal regulators are looking into a White House worker who bet on President Donald Trump's speeches.

title: 5 things to watch for in Trump's primetime address on elections
shortDescription: President Trump is set to address "election integrity" in a primetime address July 16. Here are five things to watch for.

title: White House defends ICE vehicle stops
shortDescription: White House press secretary Karoline Leavitt said ICE vehicle stops will continue as officials address recent deaths.

title: White House says CDC cuts didn't impact outbreak response
shortDescription: White House Press Secretary Karoline Leavitt said the CDC and FDA "have the resources they need" to combat an outbreak of a diarrhea-causing parasite.

title: Vance: Trump administration 'absolutely screwed up' Epstein files release
shortDescription: JD Vance told Joe Rogan that the Trump administration "screwed up" its handling of the Epstein files, acknowledging the rollout fueled mistrust.

title: Who is Roger Rogoff? Trump fires US attorney an hour into Seattle job
shortDescription: Roger Rogoff came to the helm of the U.S. attorney's office in Seattle on Wednesday. After 54 minutes and an email, his time in the position was over.

title: Permanent daylight saving has precedent. See Congress's past efforts
shortDescription: Congress is one step closer to making daylight saving time permanent. But it's not the first time this has happened.
